level_signals: ex-founder with strong product instincts listed as equivalent
Ownership of platform primitives across all Supabase products and enterprise deals

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have 7+ years of product management experience on developer tools, or an ex-founder with strong product instincts.
must_haves.qualifier: developer tools or equivalent ex-founder experience

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You're technical enough to dive in: read an architecture doc, follow a design discussion, ask the right questions.
Have shipped cloud infrastructure as a product. You've owned at least one of compute, networking, storage, or multi-region/multi-tenant systems at a cloud, infra, or PaaS company.

You understand the tradeoffs well enough to judge them: instance sizing and capacity, disk and storage economics, scale-to-zero vs. always-on, single-tenant vs. shared, the gap between "works in one region" and "works in twenty."
Are biased toward speed. You'd rather ship something imperfect and learn than spend another sprint refining a spec.

Build with what you own. You run real workloads on the products you own: standing up a read replica, putting a custom domain in front of a project, terminating traffic through PrivateLink, deploying onto BYOC.
Use AI to move faster. You use AI tools to cut the slow parts so more time goes to judgment calls.
Work async by default. You make decisions in writing and move threads forward without meetings.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Familiarity with how enterprises buy infrastructure (PrivateLink, BYOC, procurement) is a bonus.

scope.surface: Platform primitives: compute, disks, networking, API gateway, read replicas, custom domains, PrivateLink, Bring Your Own Cloud
scope.business_model: open-source developer platform, B2D and B2B enterprise
core_responsibilities: Talk to customers across the full spectrum — indie developers, startups, enterprises, and partners — and bring real blockers back to the roadmap
Own the problem statement and requirements behind every platform bet
Decide what gets built, what gets deferred, and what gets cut each quarter
Define how each launch is measured before it ships and track results afterward
Keep engineering, design, and leadership aligned by writing the roadmap and surfacing dependencies

compensation.equity_form: ESOP (equity ownership) — every team member receives ESOP

green_flags: Fully remote-global with no office requirement
Comprehensive benefits: 100% health insurance for employees, ESOP, tech allowance, education allowance
Well-funded company ($500M raised) with 7M+ developer users
Async-first culture clearly articulated in job description
Encourages ex-founders as equivalent candidates to 7+ year PMs
Open-source-first mission signals authentic developer focus
red_flags: Visa sponsorship status not mentioned — unclear for global applicants
Very broad platform scope (touches every Supabase product, every region, every customer tier) may indicate high complexity and pressure
7+ years minimum is a high bar that may exclude strong mid-level candidates

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 7+ years requirement and scope of platform ownership)
seniority.manages_people (not stated; inferred false from IC-level responsibilities described)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred true from 'hire globally' and 'fully remote' statements)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred false from 'hire globally' framing with no mention of geo-adjusted pay)
ambiguities: The JD says 'Have 7+ years of product management experience on developer tools, or an ex-founder with strong product instincts' — it is unclear whether ex-founder experience fully replaces the 7+ year requirement or is an additional qualifier. Treated as an alternative path (OR condition) here.
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD. work_auth set to 'open' based on global hiring language, but sponsorship status is 'unclear'.
The 'skill_type' for dogfooding/hands-on product use does not map perfectly to any single enum; classified as capability but tagged as 'hands_on_use' evidence.
